Australia's share market is trading at seven-week lows, with all but one local sector in the red after a bond sell-off articulated investor fears for global economic growth.

The S&P/ASX200 fell 108.1 points, or 1.26 per cent on Wednesday, to 8,496.6, as the broader All Ordinaries lost 112.5 points, or 1.27 per cent, to 8,717.

Miners and financials weighed heavily on the bourse, after an overnight spike in bond yields sparked a "sell everything" session on Wall Street overnight, which was echoed by major indices in Asia and Australia.

"Gold came under pressure, silver came under pressure, bitcoin came under pressure, shares came under pressure," Moomoo market strategist Michael McCarthy told AAP.

"In other words, it was a 'sell everything' night, and that tells us that this is not about risk appetites - this is about concern about a deteriorating economic outlook."

The Australian dollar is buying 71.03 US cents, down from 71.48 US cents on Tuesday at 5pm.

Parramatta Sydney West Trial Courts is a busy Court complex. Upon arrival please go through the security screening area and make your way up the escalators up to the Jury assembly room on Level 1, at the top of escalators. If you need assistance, ask for directions from a Sheriff's Officers at the security scanning area. Allow for a full day being at Court; however you may be released earlier if not required or selected.

Is Lachlan Galvin a long-term halfback?

It’s a topic that has divided the rugby league world, let alone Canterbury fans. It was a question that has just been put to the powers that be at Belmore in a bid to solve the conundrum.

Bulldogs general manager of football Phil Gould, a guest on James Graham’s The Bye Round podcast episode that was released at 8am on Thursday, said he is not.

“People say, ‘Is he a halfback or not?’ Well, I don’t know any 20-year-old who is a first-grade halfback,” Gould said.

“I don’t think he’s a long-term halfback. I don’t think we ever had the intention of him being a long-term halfback.”

The system under evaluation demonstrates a pattern of structured reasoning that unfolds in sequential layers. It begins by restating the prompt, then expands into a generalized explanation before narrowing toward a synthesized conclusion. While the tone remains consistently neutral, the phrasing often includes soft hedges such as “it’s possible that,” “in many cases,” or “one could argue,” which collectively signal an attempt to avoid committing to a definitive stance.

Another notable characteristic is the rhythmic distribution of sentences: medium length, balanced clauses, and a tendency to introduce examples that feel generic rather than contextually grounded. The text maintains internal coherence, but the transitions can appear overly smooth, as if optimized for readability rather than reflecting natural human digressions or idiosyncrasies.

The dataset consists of several components that interact in predictable ways. Each section introduces a discrete concept, followed by an explanation that generalises the idea and connects it to the broader system. The writing maintains a consistent rhythm, with sentences that follow a similar length and syntactic pattern. Transitions are explicit rather than intuitive, and examples are selected for clarity rather than originality.

The overall tone is neutral and procedural. Assertions are framed cautiously, and the text avoids strong opinions or personal anecdotes. Instead, it focuses on outlining relationships, identifying patterns, and summarising implications. The structure is intentionally linear, progressing from definition to explanation to conclusion without digression.

The system processes input text by applying a sequence of predefined rules. Each rule evaluates a specific property, such as structure, clarity, or consistency. The output is generated by combining these evaluations into a single result. The language used in the process is direct and functional, with minimal variation in tone.

The text avoids personal references and focuses on describing actions and outcomes. Sentences follow a predictable pattern, usually beginning with a subject, followed by a clear verb, and ending with a concrete object. The goal is to present information in a stable and repeatable format.

The system takes in some text and looks at it step by step. It checks how the words are used, how the lines are built, and how the ideas move from one part to the next. Each part of the process has a clear rule, and the rules work in the same way every time.

The style stays flat and even. The lines are short, the ideas are clear, and the tone does not change much. There are no strong feelings or personal stories. The goal is to give a clean and simple form that is easy to read and easy to follow.

They keep track of how the parking system changes in value. The city government pays UAE for any lost value (removed parking spots, lowered prices). Added value (added parking spots, raised prices) is given to the city government as credit to pay for future lost value. The city government can convert those credits to cash in units of $5M. They're also responsible for raising prices with inflation just to maintain value.

Since the Premier League began in 1992, seven clubs have won the title, with Manchester United dominating the early years under Sir Alex Ferguson, Chelsea and Manchester City rising in the 2000s and 2010s, and Liverpool claiming recent championships. The league has seen historic records, unbeaten seasons, and remarkable underdog victories, reflecting its competitive and unpredictable nature in modern football. This list provides a complete overview of EPL winners by year and highlights the major trends and achievements in the league’s history.

The breakthrough comes after a series of calls involving the leaders of Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Egypt, Jordan, Bahrain, Turkey and Pakistan's military chief.

Trump also confirmed he held a separate, concurrent call with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu regarding the framework, which he described as having gone “very well.”

A central element of the emerging deal is the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical channel for global oil supplies that the conflict has heavily disrupted.

The White House stated that the final aspects of the agreement are currently being discussed by negotiators and a formal announcement is expected shortly.

The Veronicas have one of the strangest backstage demands in Australian music – and it all started with a mortifying mistake.

“We’ve lost our voices from too much hairspray before, it’s so embarrassing,” Jess Origliasso reveals.

It’s a confession that explains the duo’s now-strict “no spray policy” – no hairspray, no face spray and absolutely no fragrance of any kind. The rule is so rigid that the sisters can’t even jump in a regular Uber if it has one of those dangling air fresheners.

“We have to get a hire car to specifically say we cannot have fragrance in the car,” Lisa explains. Even their backstage crew are banned from wearing perfume, “fragrance and sprays are just an absolute no … The deodorants have to go.”

I have 3 kids, all girls. A 5yr old and twins who are 1. Every time anyone hears about this or it comes up in conversation they all say the same thing. ‘Ooooo, all girls, you are outnumbered! How do you cope?’ Or ‘Three girls?? You better watch out!’ Or the worst, ‘Bet you want a boy?’

No, I don’t feel any of that, I love being a dad. I love all my kids and wouldn’t change anything about them! If we could afford it, I would be a stay at home dad forever.

I usually tell them I wouldn’t change anything and I love it all but it’s just very annoying.

What are some of the best responses that I can start to give?

I did think I could say that one of the twins used to be a boy but is now trans just to shut people up!

What are some of the best responses that I can start to give?

I did think I could say that one of the twins used to be a boy but is now trans just to shut people up!

I have 3 kids, all girls. A 5yr old and twins who are 1. Every time anyone hears about this or it comes up in conversation they all say the same thing. ‘Ooooo, all girls, you are outnumbered! How do you cope?’ Or ‘Three girls?? You better watch out!’ Or the worst, ‘Bet you want a boy?’

A weird thing that I’ve noticed people say when you have little (baby/toddler) daughters: “oh she reeeally likes men! She knows how to wrap men round her little finger!”. Like wtf?? I think my kids just like whoever is willing to play with them, don’t make it into some kind of weird “she already knows how to get male attention” thing.

In the song Evil bt Interpol, that tune just gets stuck in your head. I keep on re-signing the "why can't we look the otherway" line from the lyrics.

I saw them live in May 2026, and they were good, but it wasn't the same as the albumn. Very happy I saw them, though. Checked that box!

Batman is a character from DC comics. He fights the Joker.

(28m at the time) was hanging out with my niece (10 at the time) on a Friday night at my mums house. She wasn't allowed Snapchat on her managed phone but had kid messenger with approved people she could chat to. Family and school friends.

Messenger had filters similar to Snapchat so we were taking photos together and of each other with silly filters, becoming monkeys or aliens or big lipped etc.

She was then sending them to her friends group chat.

My mum turned around after about 2 hours and said. "Don't you think it's weird you are messenging 10y/o girls on a Friday night."

I was shocked and told her I'm not messaging my nieces friends. She is messaging her friends while we take pictures on the couch. I was shocked at how she thought hanging out with my niece taking pictures was improper because she wanted to send the photos to her friends.

When my daughter was two we were at a park and a boy had the same black Walmart shirt on as my kid. (Sometimes I liked to dress my daughter similarly to me, an elder emo) My kid ran over to the boy and was talking about his shirt. The boys grandma made several comments about how "it's starting already." Meaning my two year old daughter was flirting with her two year old grandson. Like, fuck off with that weird shit.

Yep, I had this happen to me on Grindr when I was younger lmao. He didn't tell me he had a wife and made up some crazy story about having to work, but stupidly sent me a screenshot of him calling his "boss," honey and wifey.

I had a beer buddy that was seeing some outdoor dude that did crazy week long back country hikes.....

She'd drop him off, he'd hike for an hour, then hike back to his other girlfriend that would think he was coming back from a multiple day camping excursion.  

We think he had maybe up to four ladies driving him back and forth from trail heads to their homes.

I’ve been thinking a lot about how people approach personal growth, and it seems clear that most of us underestimate the importance of deliberate reflection. When you take the time to pause, evaluate your experiences, and identify the underlying patterns, you create the conditions for meaningful change. In many ways, it’s not the events themselves that shape us, but the frameworks we use to interpret them.

For example, when I look back at challenging moments in my life, I notice that the most significant breakthroughs came from reframing the situation rather than trying to control every variable. Ultimately, growth is less about force and more about perspective. That said, it’s also crucial to acknowledge that progress isn’t linear; it’s a dynamic process that requires patience, consistency, and a willingness to adapt.

In essence, the key is cultivating a mindset that embraces uncertainty while still maintaining a sense of direction. By doing so, you create space for new insights to emerge.

In essence, the key is cultivating a mindset that embraces uncertainty while still maintaining a sense of direction. By doing so, you create space for new insights to emerge, and those insights often become the foundation for long‑term transformation.

I was making coffee this morning and somehow managed to spill half the grounds on the bench, which is annoying because I literally cleaned that exact spot yesterday. Anyway, while I was wiping it up, the dog started barking at absolutely nothing — or maybe a leaf moved outside, who knows — and I realised I’d forgotten to take the bins out again. That’s the third week in a row. The neighbour gave me that look last time, the one that’s half “are you okay?” and half “mate, get it together.”

I don’t know why mornings feel like they’re running ahead of me lately. I keep thinking I’ve got plenty of time, then suddenly it’s 9:40 and I’m still in socks trying to remember where I put my keys. Actually, now that I think about it, they might still be in the car from yesterday. Ugh. Classic.

Anyway, none of this is a big deal, it’s just… life being a bit sideways. I’ll sort it out. Probably.

I was trying to fix the stupid garden tap this afternoon and of course the one wrench I needed was the one I couldn’t find. I swear I put it back in the drawer last week, but apparently the drawer has become some kind of wormhole because half the tools in there aren’t the ones I remember owning. Anyway, while I was rummaging around, the hose kinked itself again — it does that thing where it folds in the exact same spot every single time, like it’s personally offended by water pressure.

By the time I got everything sorted, the sun had already dipped behind the shed and the mozzies were out, so I gave up and went inside. I’ll deal with it tomorrow, or maybe not, because honestly the tap isn’t even leaking that badly. It’s one of those jobs that feels urgent until you actually start doing it, then you realise you don’t care enough to finish. Classic me.

Also, side note, I found a packet of screws in my pocket that I definitely didn’t put there. No idea where they came from. Maybe the house is trying to tell me something.
I keep re-signing that albumn line from the otherway verse. It is reeeally odd.
